title: China's Coal Imports From Russia Are Likely To Continue to Climb
section_path: China's Coal Imports From Russia Are Likely To Continue to Climb
section_path_text: China's Coal Imports From Russia Are Likely To Continue to Climb
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
China's coal import data for May by origin has been released and shows imports from Indonesia have remained the primary source of imports and Russia the second largest source of imports. China's coal imports from Indonesia totaled 12.4 million tons in May, which is down from April by 3.4 million tons (-23%) but up year-on-year by 1.2 million tons (11%). Image reference: Chart 1 (37).jpg | assets/2022-07-04_chinas-coal-imports-from-russia-are-likely-to-continue-to-climb_img_chart1283729_42c1c02f21c5.jpg
China's coal imports from Russia totaled 5 million tons in May, which is up from April by 1.1 million tons (28%) but down year-on-year by 100,000 tons (-2%).
